codeReview.summary: Core API key endpoints and tests were added, but the implementation does not fully meet the feature contract for hashing and scope enforcement.

issues.description: API keys are stored using hashToken (SHA-256) instead of PBKDF2. The feature requires PBKDF2 hashing for API keys, so VAL-ID-005 is not implemented as specified.

Scope checks are only applied on /shares GET/POST. Read endpoints like /spikes and /spikes/:id authenticate via validateAdminOrUserToken without hasScope checks, so write-scoped API keys can still perform read operations, violating scoped key behavior.

DELETE/PATCH /auth/api-key/:key_id authorization checks ownership but never enforces auth.scopes. A read-scoped API key can perform write actions (revoke/update), which breaks read/write/full scope guarantees.

sharedStateObservations.observation: worker-backend skill says to write tests first, but this session edited production schema/constants before creating the new api-keys test file. skillFeedback still marked followedProcedure=true.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton sequence for workerSessionId 0f10df91-2205-4bfb-89ff-482975bc837f shows schema edits (schema.sql/schema.ts/constants) before creating worker/test/api-keys.test.ts.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared state has an API-key hashing convention drift: docs/instructions state PBKDF2 for API-key hashing, while implementation explicitly uses SHA-256.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Mission AGENTS.md and .factory/library/architecture.md describe PBKDF2-based API key hashing; worker/src/handlers/api-keys.ts:98-102 stores keyHash via hashToken (SHA-256).

summary: Reviewed handoff, commit 667524c diff, and transcript skeleton for session 0f10df91-2205-4bfb-89ff-482975bc837f. Result is FAIL due to blocking gaps in required PBKDF2 key hashing and comprehensive scope enforcement for scoped API keys.
